directive_summary.title: State-file archive architecture review — separate live state from historical record across the whole pipeline
directive_summary.outcome: PROP-022 authored and committed: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-022-state-file-archive-architecture.json (~78KB, 9 deliverable structure sections, all 6 open design questions answered, 11 files with per-file migration plans ranked by risk).
directive_summary.directive_status_after_run: completed (status field flipped from 'pending' to 'completed' per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception)

biggest_offenders_by_daily_token_cost: monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json — 1.4MB × 35 reads/day = 49MB/day token cost; 100% archive (no live state)
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json — 50KB × 16 reads/day = 800KB/day; 981B live of 50KB (PROP-021 canonical example)

ranked_by_risk_low_to_high: monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json (rank 11 — highest risk: 1.4MB, 4 readers, lookup-by-id critical)

consumers: PROP-009r2 3-day rolling audit — exact code change provided (streamed read of priority-queue-archive.jsonl filtered by popped_at >= cutoff; replaces fixed-200-cap)
Decider lifecycle verification — exact code change provided (event-sourcing pattern: status updates append new lines; readers grep id | tail -1)
Integrity coverage checks — exact code change provided (two-pass over live + archive for collision detection)
Tinker's own audit lenses — UNCHANGED + ENHANCED (gain bloat-regression check)
Operator workflow — UNCHANGED (always writes to live file; agent moves to archive on consumption)

obsolete_justification_documented.PROP-009r2_history_cap_200: Documented: original justification (50-item backlog April 2026), what changed (queue currently 0, throughput 3-4/day), new sizing in archive era (no cap; audit reader filters by popped_at >= cutoff against full archive). Code-removal target: monitor/prompts/decider.md line 370-371.

open_design_questions_addressed.q1_single_vs_per_event_files: SINGLE FILE PER SOURCE (rationale: operationally simpler, FUSE-compatible, grep-friendly)
open_design_questions_addressed.q2_rotation_vs_unbounded: UNBOUNDED DEFAULT, rotation when single archive >10MB (mechanism: writeFileSync('') truncate to history file; no rename, FUSE-compatible)
open_design_questions_addressed.q3_fuse_no_unlink: FULLY COMPATIBLE — never unlinks any archive file
open_design_questions_addressed.q4_integrity_bloat_lint: YES — add lint-state-file-bloat.js daily (deferrable to separate PROP)
open_design_questions_addressed.q5_operator_workflow: ALWAYS APPEND TO LIVE — uniform across all human-notes files
open_design_questions_addressed.q6_files_lacking_distinction: Apply operator's rule: if read on every dispatcher cycle and not iterated in fast path, file is pure archive. closed-issues, processed-reviews qualify.

work_performed.PROP_018_classifications_proposed: 10 new sync-manifest entries proposed (one per archive file). Direction: workspace_to_clone. Classification: 'log' for ID-lookup-light, 'ledger' for ID-lookup-critical (closed-issues, expansion-tracker).

estimated_impact_summary.daily_token_savings: ~1.7M tokens/day across all dispatcher reads (analyst 960K + curmudgeon 560K + decider 180K + social 11K)
estimated_impact_summary.wall_clock_savings: ~3.5 seconds/day total (negligible at daily aggregate; meaningful per-run for tail-latency-sensitive paths)
estimated_impact_summary.disk_space: ~0 net change (archives mirror what leaves live files)
estimated_impact_summary.maintenance_cost: ONE convention documented in state-file-archives.md; new state files inherit by default; tinker Mode 3 gains bloat-regression lint

previous_followup: Walked monitor/tinker/proposals/ for prior PROPs and verified state. PROP-021 (curmudgeon dispatcher optimization) is pending-operator-review per its status field; this PROP-022 is its broadened sibling (Option C done right). PROP-018 (sync-manifest) is pending-operator-review; PROP-022 explicitly proposes 10 new sync-manifest entries that PROP-018's manifest will absorb when both land. PROP-019/PROP-020 are out-of-scope per directive non_goals (verified: PROP-022 does not touch monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/* or monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json). No verification_pattern grep needed for this run because PROP-022 is propose-only — no code applied yet, so no STILL_BROKEN-vs-FIXED determination is meaningful for this PROP itself.

findings.title: Schema cleanup of processed-reviews.json deferred per directive non_goals
findings.description: monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json carries four overlapping arrays (processed:457, section_reviews_processed:2, holistic_reviews_processed:5, processed_reviews:71, processed_files:1) — only the 'processed' array is canonical per DATA-SCHEMAS.md. The legacy duplication is a separate cleanup target. PROP-022 only addresses the live/archive split; schema cleanup remains.
findings.recommended_action: Future PROP after PROP-022 lands: schema-dedup processed-reviews to a single canonical array. Out of scope for PROP-022.

findings.title: open-issues.json hygiene leak observed but not fixed
findings.description: monitor/decisions/open-issues.json contains 5 entries with terminal status (closed/fixed/wontfix) that should have been moved to closed-issues. ISS-1432, ISS-1433 (closed), ISS-1448 (wontfix), ISS-1569 (fixed), ISS-1697 (fixed). This is a decider hygiene bug, not an architecture bug — open-issues.json is correctly designed as a live-only file.
findings.recommended_action: Decider intake should sweep open-issues for terminal-status entries and move them to closed-issues. Out of scope for PROP-022; flag as a future decider-side fix.

no_op_summary: Pure proposal authorship per directive. NO state files modified. NO prompt files modified. NO scripts created. The only writes performed by this run: (1) the new PROP-022 proposal file, (2) this report, (3) the latest-tinker-summary, (4) the directive status flip from 'pending' to 'completed' (additive edit per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE exception).
